items.prompt: Binary → Gray is commonly computed as…
items.explain: Gray = B XOR (B shifted right by 1).

items.prompt: Adjacent Gray codes flip how many bits?
items.explain: The defining property is a single-bit change.

items.prompt: Async FIFO pointers often use Gray code because…
choices: Only one bit changes across a synchronizer
items.explain: Single-bit transitions are safer across clock domains.

items.prompt: You should do normal arithmetic directly on Gray-encoded bits.
items.explain: Decode to binary (or use Gray-aware methods) before ordinary math.
